title: Sprint 1 review and retrospective on payments, observability, and open decisions.

metadata.focus: Closure of sprint 1 with verbal closures, resolved blockage, and tension over the provider decision.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: We’re wrapping up the sprint. I want to clearly distinguish what’s really done, what’s still open, and what we need to rethink for the next one.

segments.speaker: Mikel
segments.text: The structured logs are complete. In the end, they cover payments, refunds, and webhooks, and everything comes out with request_id and tenant. I consider that part closed.

segments.speaker: Jon
segments.text: From QA, I can confirm. I was able to track an incident from start to finish without having to reconstruct it manually.

segments.speaker: Mikel
segments.text: The dashboard blockage has also been resolved. Purchases renewed the telemetry license on Thursday, and I was able to deploy the 5xx error panel in production.

segments.speaker: Nora
segments.text: Support is already looking into it to see if a spike is coming from the provider or from us. That has changed our lives quite a bit.

segments.speaker: Nora
segments.text: The runbook for error codes is also closed. We’ve resolved several tickets without having to ask backend anything.

segments.speaker: Leire
segments.text: We’ve connected the timeout alerts to the channel that operations already had. It was indeed a duplicate, so we didn’t set up a new system, but the need is covered.

segments.speaker: Jon
segments.text: The minimum smoke suite is also done and runs on every payments deploy. It doesn’t cover everything, but we’re not completely flying blind anymore.

segments.speaker: Mikel
segments.text: The payment processing endpoint that was hanging hasn’t been fully resolved. We already know that the problem comes from a bad retry strategy against the provider, but the fix with backoff will have to wait for the next sprint.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: Okay, so that task remains open and will carry over to the next sprint as a priority.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: There’s also the issue of the secondary provider. In planning, we decided to keep the current provider during this sprint while we set up observability. Now we have data.

segments.speaker: Mikel
segments.text: And precisely because of that data, I’m no longer comfortable just keeping it as is. We’ve seen some really ugly spikes of 5xx on Mondays, and I think we should start talking to another option as soon as possible.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: That contradicts the decision we started the sprint with. I’m not saying you’re wrong, I’m just saying we need to record it as a change of decision and not as continuity.

segments.speaker: Leire
segments.text: I wouldn’t open a migration just yet, but I would suggest a formal comparison in the next sprint. At least now we’re not giving opinions blindly.

segments.speaker: Jon
segments.text: That works for me. We’re not closing off whether we change providers or not, but we are opening the evaluation with real data.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: So we’ll wrap it up like this: we’ll postpone the possible migration to the next sprint and open a formal comparison of the current provider against alternatives, based on the dashboard data.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: Pending items carrying over to sprint 2: fix the endpoint with backoff and open the provider comparison. The rest we consider closed.

expected_items.title: Postpone the possible provider migration to the next sprint and open a formal comparison.
expected_items.summary: The team decides not to migrate yet, but to analyze the current provider against alternatives with real data.

expected_items.title: Record the reopening of the debate on the current provider as a change of decision.
expected_items.summary: The stance of keeping the provider during the sprint is no longer assumed to be stable and will be explicitly reviewed.

expected_followups.matched_history_title: Add structured logs to the payment service with request_id and tenant.
Create a dashboard for 5xx errors from the checkout.
Document the provider's error codes for support.
Alert when the payment provider's timeouts increase.
Prepare a minimal smoke suite for the payment flow.
Review the payment processing endpoint that hangs in production.
Keep the current provider during the sprint while setting up observability.
